examples: Two airframe parachutes can be deployed in the event of the vehicle's catastrophic failure.
Tests by NASA with a modified fighter jet have shown that novel airframe shapes can reduce the boom.
Airlines can then choose which airframe to buy, and which engines, and there is competition in both fields.
The weight of the airframe determines, to some extent, how much fuel is needed for any one flight.
But some analysts have said the U.S. might try to arm the airframe at some point in the future.
